title: analytics — subprocessors (GDPR Art. 28)
description: Third-party analytics providers that process tenant subject data on behalf of Academorix. Each requires a signed DPA + is disclosed on the Trust Center. Tenants see this list on the Compliance Center screen; DPO exports via DSAR orchestrator.

subprocessors.name: PostHog
subprocessors.purpose: Product analytics, session recording, feature flags, A/B experimentation. Behavioral events + optionally session recordings.
data_categories: ip address (hashed by us before send)
session recording data (when enabled + functional consent)

subprocessors.name: Amplitude
subprocessors.purpose: Product analytics, behavioral cohort analysis, funnel + retention reporting.
data_categories: ip (server-side; masked to /24 by Amplitude when GDPR mode enabled)

subprocessors.name: Mixpanel
subprocessors.purpose: Product analytics, cohort analysis, funnel + retention.

subprocessors.name: Segment (Twilio)
subprocessors.purpose: Customer data platform. Upstream fan-out point — Segment routes to N downstream destinations configured tenant-side in Segment console (Amplitude, Mixpanel, warehouse BigQuery, ...).

subprocessors.name: June
subprocessors.purpose: B2B product analytics oriented around company-level insights + retention curves.

subprocessors.name: Google Analytics 4 (Google)
subprocessors.purpose: Web + app analytics via GA4 measurement protocol. Behavioral events + user + device context.
subprocessors.note: Google Analytics 4 appears in both `analytics` and `marketing` module subprocessors. Same processor, different event categories.

custom_webhook_note: The `custom_webhook` provider driver dispatches to caller-configured endpoints. It is NOT a first-party subprocessor — the tenant is the controller of whatever endpoint they configure. Compliance responsibility for that endpoint's data handling lies with the tenant, not Academorix. Egress domain validation (tenant.allowed_egress_domains) is the tenant's guardrail.
